name: Jay Livingston
abstract: Jay Livingston (born Jacob Harold Levison; March 28, 1915 – October 17, 2001) was an American composer best known as half of a composing-songwriting duo with Ray Evans, with whom he specialized in composing film scores and original soundtrack songs. Livingston composed the music while Evans wrote the lyrics.

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: People had told me that before like…Jay Livingston and Ray Evans had a song in a C picture... which was probably one of the worst pictures ever made and the song was, “Mona Lisa,” for which they won the Academy Award
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Weber
about_quotes.evidence: you did that with Jay Livingston and Ray Evans, guys who were born six weeks apart in 1950.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Weber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 2, 2012
